extraction.one_line_summary: TEUNS Dylan won after Valverde led into final Mur de Huy hairpins but cracked on 18-19% gradients, Bahrain's Belgian first home winner since Gilbert.
tactical_patterns: Geraint Thomas drove tempo on final lap for Ineos with 9km to go, neutralizing late breakaway for sprinters.
Søren Kragh Andersen's attack between climbs reached Mur de Huy base first, forcing favorites to chase from behind.
Enric Mas positioned Valverde perfectly entering Mur de Huy, but Valverde cracked on steepest 18-19% hairpins specifically.

rider_signals.PIDCOCK Tom: negative: dropped on Côte de Cherave/Ahffe, still affected by recent illness issues before finale
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: negative: had perfect Mas positioning but couldn't hold power on steepest hairpin gradients despite five prior wins
rider_signals.VLASOV Alexandr: positive: third place finish unexpected for Russian, suggests strong form on explosive steep gradients
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: negative: fourth place as defending champion seeking fourth win indicates declining form on Mur de Huy
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: neutral: worked final lap tempo control for Ineos but unclear if for himself or teammate
rider_signals.TEUNS Dylan: positive: won without leadout support, timed surge on steepest hairpins perfectly when Valverde faded
